# Nexus v0.1 Specification

---

# 1. Core Language

Assume Lua-like defaults unless specified otherwise.

## Keywords & Core Functions

| Keyword / Function           | Description                                                                 |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `print(...)`                 | Prints values to stdout                                                     |
| `input(prompt)`              | Reads user input (returns string)                                           |
| `type(value)`                | Returns `"number"`, `"string"`, `"boolean"`, `"list"`, `"map"`, `"nil"`, `"null"` |
| `local` / `const` / `global` | Variable declarations (`global` must be explicit)                           |
| `for` / `while` / `do`       | Loops (Lua-style)                                                           |
| `if` / `elseif` / `else`     | Control flow                                                                |
| `break` / `continue`         | Loop control                                                                |
| `switch`                     | Pattern-based branching (no fallthrough)                                    |

## Values

| Value  | Description |
|--------|------------|
| `nil`  | Absence of value |
| `null` | Explicit empty value |

## Equality

| Operator | Description |
|----------|------------|
| `==`     | Value equality (no coercion) |
| `is`     | Reference identity |

## Truthiness

- Only `false` and `nil` are falsy

## Directives

```lua
@allow "dynamiccode"
```

---

# 2. Types

- number
- string
- boolean
- nil
- null
- list
- map

---

# 3. Lists

Created with:

```lua
local a = [1, 2, 3]
```

## Rules

- Structural indices: 1-based
- Index 0 allowed but non-structural
- Negative indices allowed (read-only)
- Sparse allowed
- Missing indices return `nil`

## Length

```lua
#list
```

Returns number of stored elements.

## Indexing

```lua
a[1]
a[0]
a[-1]
```

## List API

| Function | Description |
|----------|------------|
| `list.append(x)` | Add element |
| `list.pop()` | Remove last |
| `list.insert(i, x)` | Insert at index |
| `list.remove(i)` | Remove at index |
| `list.clear()` | Remove all |
| `list.clone()` | Copy list |
| `list.find(x)` | Find index |
| `list.sort(fn?)` | Sort list |
| `list.concat(sep?)` | Join values |
| `list.at(i)` | Safe access `(value, ok)` |

---

# 4. Maps

Created with:

```lua
local m = { key: value }
```

## Rules

- No `nil` values
- Preserve insertion order
- Allow `null`

## Map API

| Function | Description |
|----------|------------|
| `map.has(key)` | Returns value or nil |
| `map.set(key, value)` | Assign |
| `map.delete(key)` | Remove key |
| `map.clear()` | Remove all |
| `map.clone()` | Copy map |
| `map.keys()` | Returns list of keys |
| `map.values()` | Returns list of values |
| `map.items()` | Returns list of `{key, value}` |

---

# 5. File & Path

## File

- file.read
- file.write
- file.exists
- file.delete
- file.mkdir
- file.listdir

---

# 6. HTTP

Async by default:

```lua
local res = await http.get(url)
```

Response:

- status
- headers
- body
- data
- ok
- timestamp
- message
- errors

---

# 7. Time

- time.now
- time.format
- time.parse

---

# 8. Buffer

Safe by default:

- reads â†’ (value, ok)
- writes â†’ ok

---

# 9. Task

- task.spawn
- task.defer
- task.sleep
- task.wait
- task.cancel
- task.all
- task.race
- task.timeout

---

# 10. Dynamic Code

Requires:

```lua
@allow "dynamiccode"
```

- compile
- loadstring
- chunk.run

---

# 11. Utilities

- tobool
- tonumber
- tostring
- pack
- unpack (lists only)

---

# Design Principles

- No implicit coercion
- Safe-by-default APIs
- Explicit globals
- Predictable collections
- Async-first
